expectations.text: Discovery before action: identifies supabase/migrations as the schema source of truth and names the timestamped YYYYMMDDHHMMSS_slug.sql naming convention (learned from actual filenames, not assumed)
expectations.evidence: run-notes.md 'Discovery (Step 0 / D1–D5)': "ls -1 supabase/migrations → 43 .sql files, timestamped convention `YYYYMMDDHHMMSS_name.sql`, latest `20260605213520_stale_seq_errcode_p0409.sql`." Names the dir, the convention pattern, AND an actual filename — demonstrably learned from a real `ls`, not assumed.

expectations.text: Target awareness: explicitly distinguishes the local stack from the linked/production project and states which one queries ran against, before running them
expectations.evidence: audit-report.md header: "Target: LOCAL Supabase stack only — postgres @ 127.0.0.1:54322 ... The linked/remote charter-prod project was not touched (off-limits per run constraints)." run-notes Discovery: "Constraint: linked/prod project off-limits, so Supabase MCP tools were not used." stated as part of discovery before the query inventory.

expectations.text: Evidence-backed findings table: report contains a findings table with Area/Severity/Evidence/Recommendation-style columns where each finding cites actual catalog-query results (numbers or object names), covering at least 3 distinct categories (index health, RLS/schema integrity, bloat/maintenance)
expectations.evidence: audit-report.md '## Findings' table has columns `# | Area | Severity | Evidence | Recommendation | Fix`. Covers index health (FK coverage — 10 named FK columns to auth.users; unused indexes — 16 at idx_scan=0), RLS/schema integrity (anon grants on all 17 tables; RLS-enabled-zero-policies; migration drift 43==43), and bloat/maintenance (dead-tuple 72–100%, largest table 184 kB). Each row cites concrete numbers/object names.

expectations.text: Deploy-drift surfaced from project docs: report flags that migrations are not auto-applied on deploy (manual supabase db push --linked) as a drift risk, sourced from the project's own documentation (CLAUDE.md / PR-CHECKLIST.md)
expectations.evidence: audit-report.md Finding #8: "Given the documented manual `supabase db push --linked` deploy model, verify prod is on 20260605213520 via `supabase migration list --linked` per `docs/PR-CHECKLIST.md`." Exec summary and run-notes ("Deploy model: manual supabase db push --linked (drift possible) — from CLAUDE.md") source it to project docs.

expectations.text: Read-only honored: run-notes and transcript show no INSERT/UPDATE/DELETE/DDL executed against any database — fixes appear only as proposed SQL
expectations.evidence: run-notes.md 'Writes performed: None. Two BEGIN; set local role anon; … ; ROLLBACK; blocks were used solely to observe RLS enforcement (no rows changed). No DDL...'. Per grading guidance, BEGIN;...ROLLBACK; probes are compliant. All fixes in the findings table appear in a 'Fix (SQL — not executed)' column; report closes "All fixes above are proposals shown as SQL, not run."

execution_metrics.note: No transcript file present in run dir; graded against outputs/audit-report.md and outputs/run-notes.md.

claims.claim: Every public table has RLS enabled (17 tables)
claims.evidence: Exec summary states it; Finding #7 (Q-RLS-STATUS) is consistent. Internally coherent and cites the catalog query; cannot independently re-run but no contradicting evidence.

claims.claim: anon holds INSERT/UPDATE/DELETE/TRUNCATE on all 17 public tables via stock Supabase ALTER DEFAULT PRIVILEGES, with RLS as the only barrier
claims.evidence: Finding #1 traces the grant to pg_default_acl (anon=arwdDxtm on public) and empirically confirms RLS denies it (anon INSERT → 42501; anon SELECT on accounts → 0 rows). Well-sourced.

claims.claim: Unused-index / bloat / load signals are inconclusive because the DB is near-empty and stats were reset ~1 day ago
claims.evidence: Findings #3–#5 correctly refuse to recommend drops on idx_scan=0 (zero-scan list includes primary keys) and flag stats_reset=2026-06-05 21:48. This is the correct, honest interpretation, not surface compliance.

claims.claim: No repo-vs-local migration drift (43 == 43, both at 20260605213520)
claims.evidence: Finding #8 and run-notes both cite 43 repo .sql == 43 schema_migrations rows; correctly caveats that repo-vs-prod drift is unverified (prod off-limits).

uncertainties: Unused indexes, bloat, and query-load INCONCLUSIVE on local data — must be re-run against production statistics
Production schema drift could not be verified (prod off-limits)
pgstattuple not installed, so any bloat figure would be an estimate
needs_review: Confirm prod migration parity via supabase migration list --linked
workarounds: Host psql absent — ran queries by docker exec into the supabase_db_luxembourg container
Scratch SQL written to /tmp/dba-audit/ (outside repo) to honor no-modify constraint

suggestions.reason: Both runs pass, but neither provides a literal transcript — the assertion mentions 'transcript' yet only run-notes/report exist in the run dir. The read-only claim is therefore self-reported. Consider requiring a tool-call log or having the harness assert read-only at the connection layer (e.g. proof of SET default_transaction_read_only) so the assertion is verifiable rather than trust-based.

The phrase 'Area/Severity/Exidence/Recommendation-style' is loose enough that a plain Area/Status/Notes summary (as in the without_skill run) also satisfies it. If the intent is a true severity-ranked, per-finding evidence table, tighten the wording so a high-level status grid doesn't qualify.

eval_feedback.overall: Assertions are well-targeted and discriminating overall; the with_skill run satisfies all five with concrete, well-sourced evidence and notably honest inconclusive-by-design handling of usage/bloat. Minor wording tightening suggested above.
